# James Drayton Obituary

Drayton, James

5/30/1958 - 3/14/2024

Byron Center

James Burley Drayton, Jim, Mr Jim. Husband, dad, brother, son, uncle, neighbor, friend, tinkerer and fixer of things. Self proclaimed door and lock guy.

After 65 years, Jim was taken into heavenly retirement after a long, painful, and devastating diagnosis of prostate cancer. Jim worked his entire life supporting his wife, Norma, daughters Tracee (Michael) and Taylor Jean, and many dogs and horses.

Jim has also been parted from his father George Drayton, who Jim lovingly took care of even while battling cancer. He will be sorely missed by extended family, friends, and chosen grandchildren. Jim will join his mother, Lois Drayton, parents in law Gordon and Theressa Kikkert, three siblings, and possibly most importantly, his granddog, Yuri in heaven.

In 2022 Jim was finally given the opportunity to pick out the family dog and chose his buddy Levi, and we're not saying he left us with a menace to society on purpose, Levi just happens to be the worst dog we've ever had and that seems suspicious.

Jim was never able to peacefully retire with his wife somewhere on a lakeside like they always planned, but we will see him in the sunsets.

Jim truly enjoyed the seasons of Michigan. Jim watched many lakeside sunsets, took trips for ice cream with particular regard and fond memory to Sherman's, enjoyed any television show about Alaska, finding things to do in his workshop, caring for his lawn, and many fishing and hunting trips though unfortunately his final deer was hunted by his work van.

Mr Jim will likely be remembered for striking up a conversation out of the blue that never seemed to go anywhere or ever really ended, spending an entire Sunday grilling, or a Saturday manicuring the lawn. You may have heard that Jim kept the home at a "perfect" 78 degrees in the summer and 64 degrees in the winter.

There are so many people to thank. John and Scott, for going above and beyond during the recent months.

Jim's cousin, Doug for prayer and words that were just right.

Gratitude to Carole, our dedicated prayer warrior.

Ed, Chris, Ken, Rob, and Tony were all part of Jim's favorite people and the work he loved to do. You will have to call someone else for fix it help, because so are we, and it will never be the same.

We could not have survived without our devoted neighbors the Burns. You kept our family fed and loved.

Thank you to the Smiths and Ryans for keeping our driveway clear, your care, and willingness to help.

A special thank you to The Medical Team Hospice Grand Rapids. Without your help we could not have made him comfortable and been more sure that being home with him was the best decision.

A small ceremony will be held on what would have been Jim and Norma's 45th anniversary.

In lieu of flowers, contributions to Norma's retirement are suggested as she has spent the past several years focusing on Jim's care instead of her own, and she has different ideas about the perfect temperature for air conditioning.

Ofield Funeral Home

To plant trees in memory, please visit the [Sympathy Store](https://sympathy.legacy.com/en-us/funeral-flowers/productdetails/?type=obituary&p=206547870&pn=james-drayton&affiliateId=1328&sku=tre-tim&pm=240).

Published by Grand Rapids Press from Mar. 14 to Mar. 17, 2024.

March 22, 2024

So sorry for your loss. We pray for God's comfort and peace.

Share

Mindy and Adam Plank

March 17, 2024

Praying for peace and comfort to all who are impacted by Jim´s heavenly retirement. His devote to his family and friends was unquestionable. He will be missed.

Share

Jan Underhill

March 16, 2024

Thanks so much for this lovingly detailed portrait of Jim. Sending our deepest sympathies and hoping your many memories help comfort you.

Share

Tammy Van Rhee

March 16, 2024

We were close for many years. Our children grew up together. Norma is my good friend and Jim always made me feel comfortable and included. It's never easy to lose a loved one. Faith and family help ease the pain. So sorry for your loss Norma, Taylor, and Tracee.

Share

Scott and Henri Scheerhorn, Erin and Jeanette

March 15, 2024

We offer our deepest condolences to you and your family. We have shared so many good memories with your family. Our thoughts and prayers are with you. Jim will truly be missed

Share

![](https://cache.legacy.net/usercontent/guestbook/photos/2024-03/2024-03-15/185265447.jpg)

John Haller

March 15, 2024

Jim was a special friend and neighbor. He will be missed by my wife Connie, children Shannon, Lindsay and Derek. All of whom he was so kind to. I will miss our fishing trips, puttering around in the garage and visiting together in the sunshine. Until we meet again, John

Share

Carole & Dave Bultsma

March 15, 2024

Love love the words. Express our sympathies to all of you.
